R Notebook

# Set the folder path
folder_path <- "web-of-life_2026-03-18_071546"
file_names <- c("M_SD_003.csv", "M_SD_004.csv", "M_SD_005.csv", "M_SD_006.csv")

# Read all files and store them in a list
all_networks <- list()
for(file in file_names) {
  name <- gsub(".csv", "", file)
  all_networks[[name]] <- read.csv(file.path(folder_path, file))
  
  # Print dimensions to check
  cat(name, "dimensions:", dim(all_networks[[name]]), "\n")
}
M_SD_003 dimensions: 25 17 
M_SD_004 dimensions: 34 21 
M_SD_005 dimensions: 25 14 
M_SD_006 dimensions: 21 16 
# Method 1: Using dplyr and tidyr (recommended)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

# Convert each network to long format and combine
combined_long <- data.frame()

for(network_name in names(all_networks)) {
  df <- all_networks[[network_name]]
  
  # Get plant names from first column (assuming it's named 'X' or similar)
  plant_col <- names(df)[1]  # First column name (likely 'X')
  
  # Convert to long format
  df_long <- df %>%
    pivot_longer(cols = -all_of(plant_col),  # All columns except plant column
                 names_to = "bird_species",
                 values_to = "interaction") %>%
    rename(plant_species = all_of(plant_col)) %>%
    mutate(network = network_name)
  
  # Combine with main dataframe
  combined_long <- bind_rows(combined_long, df_long)
}

# Sum interactions by plant and bird species
merged_data <- combined_long %>%
  group_by(plant_species, bird_species) %>%
  summarise(interaction = sum(interaction, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

# Convert back to wide format (plants as rows, birds as columns)
merged_wide <- merged_data %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = bird_species,
              values_from = interaction,
              values_fill = NA)  # Use NA for missing combinations

# View the merged data
print("Merged data dimensions:")
[1] "Merged data dimensions:"
dim(merged_wide)
[1] 69 27
print(head(merged_wide, 10))
NA
NA
library(bipartite)
set.seed(123)

merged_wide <- as.data.frame(merged_wide)
rownames(merged_wide) <- merged_wide[, 1]
merged_wide <- merged_wide[, -1]

merged_wide[is.na(merged_wide)] <- 0



plotweb(merged_wide , horizontal = TRUE, spacing = 0.8, text_size = 0.5, sorting = "ca", scaling = "relative", curved_links = TRUE, higher_color = "maroon", lower_color = "maroon", link_color = "pink", link_alpha = 0.6, lower_border = "black", higher_border = "black")
